summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a fiery show with standout renditions of Box of Rain, He's Gone, The Other One and an unexpected I Know You Rider.
review: The recording is a clean, well‑balanced soundboard mix that lets Keith’s keys shine and captures Jerry’s hard‑riffs without muddiness. Musically the concert is a high‑energy romp – mellow set one builds into a powerful second set highlighted by Box of Rain, He's Gone, a blazing The Other One with an I Know You Rider out of space, and a fiery Greatest Story/Promised Land/Bertha sequence. The only blemish mentioned is a slightly over‑the‑top Sugar Magnolia ending.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed, enthusiastic review praises both the performance and the soundboard quality; weighting gives a high overall score but limited sample lowers confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, crisp mix with Keith’s keyboards nicely balanced; drums are brief but audible; no reported drop‑outs or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Set one builds mellowly and transitions smoothly into a high‑octane second set; the band takes adventurous detours (I Know You Rider out of space) while keeping strong song-to-song continuity.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Riffs hard throughout El Paso, never stops getting in his licks, and drives the intensity of The Other One.
band_member_comments.Phil: Provides solid bass riffs and a nice baritone vocal on He's Gone.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's keyboard work is noted as “nice in the mix,” adding depth to the performance.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy’s drum solo is very brief (1:33) but effective; overall percussion supports the jams.
